{{Quote|I've said it before and I'll say it again: the only good Rebel is a ''dead'' Rebel!|Dol Donar II|audio=OnlyGoodRebelDeadRebel-DFSftE.flac|<ref name="SftE">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref>}}
'''Dol Donar II''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of [[Derra IV/Legends|Derra IV]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. His wife was [[Rissa Donar]], and his youngest son, [[Nathan Donar III]], was a graduate of the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]]. He had a strong dislike of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==Biography==
Dol Donar II was the [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Derra IV/Legends|Derra IV]] by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. By this point, Donar was [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to a woman named [[Rissa Donar|Rissa]], and they had a son who was named [[Nathan Donar III]] whom Dol often advised that "only suckers take chances".

On the twentieth anniversary of their wedding, Madame Donar gave him a precious ring.

In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] the couple visited the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] on the planet [[Carida/Legends|Carida]] to attend the graduation ceremony of Nathan's class. Following this, the family took a trip on the luxury [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Star of Empire]]''. On board, Nathan met and invited fellow student [[Kyle Katarn]] to have dinner with the family, whom he introduced as his famous and heroic friend; Donar was interested in Kyle's background and was enraged hearing that [[Morgan Katarn|his father]] was killed by Rebel terrorists, assuring him that the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]] was preparing [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|something]] against them. He ignored his wife's admonition to lower his voice, and ignored her as she nodded negatively when [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] approached them for a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]].

After dinner, father and son went to the [[Corellia Room]] to join Calrissian; while at first the businessman allowed him to win the first hands to keep him in the game,<ref name="AD">[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'' audio drama]]</ref> it eventually resulted in Donar placing, and losing several hefty bets, including his wife's anniversary ring. This brought some tense hours of silence in the family, and Donar pretended to stare at a blank computer screen, wishing to avoid his wife's anger, and hoping to fix the loss of the ring. He found the diversion he wished for when [[Donar family protocol droid|their droid]] brought a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] visitor, a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Slyder]]; the Donars showed annoyed by the alien's look, including his natural Rodian odor covered by cologne, and instead of greeting or welcoming him, the governor bluntly inquired his business. He was shocked to hear that Rebels were on the ship, rising up and letting his computer drop to the floor. To prove his claims, the Rodian showed a holo-recording of a seditious conversation between some Rebel [[Agent/Legends|agents]] and [[Jan Ors|the female one]] interacting with Kyle Katarn. Furious, Donar ordered Slyder to [[Skirmish on the Star of Empire|hunt and arrest them]].

To that end he recruited some [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial military]] officers to accompany Slyder and his son, but the Rebels escaped, and a reason was that Nathan was nice enough to send a warning to his friend. They came back with their [[Droid/Legends|droid]], [[A-Cee]]. The Governor offered to employ [[Dol Donar's yacht|his own yacht]] for the pursuit, and for a reason that Slyder could not understand, he insisted on taking the droid's remains with them and had a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] to re-activate it. Slyder thought that the bulky presence of the Governor somehow disrupted the operation.

While pursuing the Rebel ''[[Truly Sorry]]'' the bullied technician reactivated the droid. A-Cee spotted Nathan's Imperial uniform, and began to self-destruct. Slyder, who already was feeling that the humans were too incompetent to be tolerated, started shooting around before A-Cee killed everyone, destroying the yacht. Governor Donar found his end in that havoc, next to his son.<ref name="SftE" />

==Personality and traits==
Donar was a strong supporter of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In addition, he was an avid card player, and greatly enjoyed the game [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] although he admitted he had poor skills.

Kyle Katarn noticed that in a single dinner the Governor ate what Kyle needed to sustain himself for a whole week. An extremely obese man with three chins and fleshy hands, he attempted to hide his shape by wearing flowing, expensive [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] in great folds; he could be seen wearing shimmering light-reflecting fabric that rustled as he moved. He sported a pinkie ring set with a five-karat [[Rol Stone]]. He also wore a monocle.

He had a nasal voice, a trait his son inherited. His eyes reminded Katarn of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]]

==Behind the scenes==
In the audio adaptation of [[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'']], he was voiced by [[Gary Groomes]].
